collection_date: Over 12 million registered as of February 2025
Women hold 14% of mobile money accounts

original_text: First major mobile money service in Ethiopia
Historic: digital > cash for first time
22% of Ethiopian adults (age 15+) reported having an account at a financial institution (Global Findex 2011).
notes: Earliest Findex wave; extends time series back to 2011 for trajectory analysis.

original_text: Approximately 30.5% of registered mobile money accounts were active (at least one transaction in the past 90 days) as of June 2024.
notes: Quantifies the registered-vs-active gap; explains why Findex account ownership grew only 3 pp despite Telebirr's 40M+ subscribers.

original_text: Total licensed mobile money agents reached approximately 750,000 by end of FY2023/24.
notes: Agent network size is a primary driver of physical access to financial services in rural Ethiopia.

original_text: NBE issued Directive No. FIS/NBE/01/2023 establishing three tiers of simplified KYC for mobile money accounts, with Tier 1 requiring only a phone number and national ID or local ID equivalent.
notes: Regulatory unlock for mass-market mobile money adoption; pillar deliberately empty per schema design.
Telebirr launched May 2021; direct positive impact on account ownership via mobile money onboarding. parent_id=EVT_0001
Telebirr drives P2P volume growth; EthSwitch reports 158% increase FY2024/25. parent_id=EVT_0001
M-Pesa entry Aug 2023 contributes to mobile money account rate doubling by 2024. parent_id=EVT_0003

source_name: GSMA State of the Industry Report
notes: Competition from M-Pesa incentivises Telebirr UX/pricing improvements, enabling USAGE growth. parent_id=EVT_0003
NFIS-II sets 70% target by 2025; policy ambition shapes regulatory environment. parent_id=EVT_0009
Fayda rollout directly drives enrollment metric; 8M → 15M in 2024–2025. parent_id=EVT_0004
Fayda lowers ID barrier disproportionately benefiting women; expected to narrow gender gap. parent_id=EVT_0004
